summary: Per-user in-app notifications (RLS-isolated, envelope-encrypted) with unread badge and mark-read, plus device push via FCM.
env: NOTIFICATIONS_FCM_PROJECT_ID — Firebase project id; unset means pushes are logged, not delivered.
NOTIFICATIONS_FCM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON — service-account key JSON (inline or file path); unset means pushes are logged, not delivered.
userSteps: Push is optional: with no FCM config the API logs pushes and the app skips device registration — in-app notifications work either way.
To enable push, create a Firebase project, add the Android/iOS apps, and place google-services.json / GoogleService-Info.plist into the app per the FlutterFire setup.
Set NOTIFICATIONS_FCM_PROJECT_ID and NOTIFICATIONS_FCM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON on the API to deliver through FCM.
In production apply the schema and RLS with `dotnet ef database update`; in Development the app creates them at startup.
